zh.Athis-Mons, Palaiseau, Seine-et-Oise, Îl: 法國i萊-德-福朗斯艾頌阿斯-蒙斯
zh.Beaumont-du-Gâtinais, Fontainebleau, Sei: 法國瑟-吉馬內豐泰布利奧
zh.lifeSummary: 當Adam de Beaumont du Gatinais I於1160年出生在法國瑟-吉馬內豐泰布利奧時，他的父親Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais36歲，母親Agnes de Soisy30歲。他於1185年與Alix de Corbeil-Beauvais在法國i萊-德-福朗斯艾頌阿斯-蒙斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1191年在以色列מחוז הצפוןעַכּוֹ享年31歲。

zh-hans.Athis-Mons, Palaiseau, Seine-et-Oise, Îl: 法国i萊-德-福朗斯艾頌阿斯-蒙斯
zh-hans.Beaumont-du-Gâtinais, Fontainebleau, Sei: 法国瑟-吉馬內豐泰布利奧
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Adam de Beaumont du Gatinais I于1160年出生在法国瑟-吉馬內豐泰布利奧时，他的父亲Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais36岁，母亲Agnes de Soisy30岁。他于1185年与Alix de Corbeil-Beauvais在法国i萊-德-福朗斯艾頌阿斯-蒙斯结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1191年在以色列מחוז הצפוןעַכּוֹ享年31岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
English (Kent): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Siegmund.
German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Adam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical. See Adam .

en.lifeSummary: When Adam de Beaumont du Gatinais I was born in 1160, in Fontainebleau, Seine-et-Marne, France, his father, Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais, was 36 and his mother, Agnes de Soisy, was 30. He married Alix de Corbeil-Beauvais in 1185, in Athis-Mons, Essonne, Île-de-France, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1191, in Acre, Northern, Israel, at the age of 31.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Adam de Beaumont du Gatinais I est né en 1160, à Fontainebleau, Seine-et-Marne, France, son père, Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Soisy, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Alix de Corbeil-Beauvais en 1185, à Athis-Mons, Essonne, Île-de-France, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1191, à Acre, Northern, Israël, à l’âge de 31 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Adam de Beaumont du Gatinais I 1160 in Fontainebleau, Seine-et-Marne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais 36 und seine Mutter Agnes de Soisy 30. Er hat 1185 in Athis-Mons, Essonne, Île-de-France, Frankreich Alix de Corbeil-Beauvais geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1191 im Alter von 31 Jahren in Acre, Northern, Israel.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Adam de Beaumont du Gatinais I è nato il nel 1160 nel Fontainebleau, Seine-et-Marne, Francia, il suo padre, Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Agnes de Soisy, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Alix de Corbeil-Beauvais nel 1185, nel Athis-Mons, Essonne, Île-de-France, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1191, nel Acre, Northern, Israele, all'età di 31 anni.

ja.Beaumont-du-Gâtinais, Fontainebleau, Sei: フランスセアイネ-エト-マルンフォンティアイネブレアウ
ja.lifeSummary: Adam de Beaumont du Gatinais Iが1160年にフランスセアイネ-エト-マルンフォンティアイネブレアウで生まれた時，父Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinaisは36歳，母Agnes de Soisyは30歳であった。1185年にフランスiレ-デ-フランスエソンアスイス-モンスでAlix de Corbeil-Beauvaisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1191年にイスラエルמחוז הצפוןעַכּוֹで31歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Adam de Beaumont du Gatinais I(이)가 1160년 프랑스 세이인-트-마니 폰테인블리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes de Soisy(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1185년 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 에손 앳히스-몬스에서 Alix de Corbeil-Beauvais(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1191년 이스라엘 מחוז הצפון עַכּוֹ에서 31세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Adam de Beaumont du Gatinais I nasceu em 1160, em Fontainebleau, Seine-et-Marne, França, seu pai, Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de Soisy, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Alix de Corbeil-Beauvais em 1185, em Athis-Mons, Essonne, Île-de-France, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1191, em Acre, Northern, Israel, com 31 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Adam de Beaumont du Gatinais I родился в 1160 г. в местности фонтеинебло, сине-ет-марн, Франция, его отцу по имени Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Soisy, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Alix de Corbeil-Beauvais в 1185 г. в местности атис-монс, эссонн, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1191 г. в местности עַכּוֹ, מחוז הצפון, Израиль в возрасте 31 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Adam de Beaumont du Gatinais I nació en 1160, en Fontainebleau, Seine-et-Marne, Francia, su papá, Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Agnes de Soisy, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Alix de Corbeil-Beauvais en 1185, en Athis-Mons, Essonne, Île-de-France, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1191, en Acre, Northern, Israel, a la edad de 31 años.
